brief_summary: Six horizontal coral bars ranked by audible delta from biggest to smallest payoff: budget plastic case (longest, brightest coral) → full-size/TKL with plastic bottom → hot-swap mid-range → budget gasket-mount → well-engineered gasket (dashed, faint) → heavy aluminum factory-foamed (shortest, dashed). Each row carries a short qualifier. Caption: '$2 mod · zero risk · reversible in five minutes'.
data_sources: pe-foam-mod.mdx § 'Which boards benefit most' — 'Budget plastic cases ... makes a noticeable difference that is immediately obvious on first keystroke. Larger layouts ... More cavity means more resonance potential. TKL boards sit between those poles ... Hot-swap boards ... zero-commitment ... Gasket-mount boards. These already isolate the plate from the case ... incremental rather than transformative on a well-engineered gasket design. The boards that benefit least are those with heavy aluminum cases ... and boards where the PCB sits directly on internal foam from the factory.'
